answer:
The entropy change for solidification at 1120 K is found by considering the entropy change at the normal melting point (1356 K) and then adjusting for the heat capacity differences of liquid and solid copper. Using \(\Delta S_{1356} = \Delta H / T = -3100 / 1356 \approx -2.286\) cal K⁻¹ mol⁻¹, and integrating \(\int (C_{p,s} - C_{p,l})/T \, dT\) from 1356 K to 1120 K gives an additional entropy change of about +0.196 cal K⁻¹ mol⁻¹, resulting in \(\Delta S_{1120} \approx -2.09\) cal K⁻¹ mol⁻¹.  

\boxed{B}
